burden.mortalityRate: Indirect; comorbidity and suicide risk lower than MDD but present

vulnerablePopulations: people with limited access to psychotherapy
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: depressed mood most days for ≥2 years (adults) plus associated symptoms and impairment; distinguish from recurrent MDD

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: low; often detected during evaluation for chronic symptoms
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; requires long-term management and psychotherapy access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS may detect through chronic complaints; confirmation and management often in mental health services
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Psychotherapy and antidepressants when indicated; treat comorbid anxiety and chronic pain
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis and limited long-term psychotherapy access
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: APS and CAPS can provide longitudinal follow-up where available
